Blanc staggers backwards, swaying. He looks like he’s about to keel over, finally succumbing to your efforts. His green skin is glistening, coated with sweat, his body seeming to sag with exhaustion...

You blink, then look at him again. He’s not sweating. His flesh is hanging looser, yes, but the cause of the sagging isn’t exhaustion: he’s melting! 

His features blur as his skin turns runny; his once muscular form becoming akin to a melted candle. You relax slightly, lowering your [pc.weaponMain], thinking the fight is done... And then the mutant lets out an enraged roar!

His form solidifies - slightly - and he swings his still very sharp claws at you! Rage and force of will are keeping him together, and it looks like he’s planning on taking you down with him!

original: The machine before you is a twisted amalgamation of JoyCo and Dhaal technologies. The top ‘half’ of her is that of a standard-issue JoyCo nursedroid. All clothing has been scoured from her pale body, her naked form on full display. Her rich pink synth hair hangs loose down past her shoulders, but fails to fully cover her overly well-endowed chest. The lower half of her body is missing, appearing to have been fused at the waist to a gargantuan naga-like portion that comprises her new form. A long, serpentine tail drags below her, six feet thick and - as far as you can tell - about fifty feet long. Most of the steel gray structure is covered in thick plates, armoring and protecting her. The inside contents of the tail are visible through thin windows along her length, and appears to be a massive vat filled with bubbling green chemicals. At the tip of her tail you see a leaking orifice, corrosive green chemicals blistering the floor below her.

original:
A weak noise, almost a whimper, escapes from the Abomination’s throat. Her shield is destroyed and her power depleted, leaving her vulnerable. 

Head rolling side-to-side, the nursedroid part of the great machine looks like she’s about to faint... and then her spine straightens and she lets out a howl of rage. The Abomination spasms, her body coming to life! 

It surges forward, and you think it’s attacking until it snakes by... heading straight for the massive power relay on the far wall. The droid pulls back her fist, then plunges it into the relay, grasping at something within!

She lets out a visceral scream, her pink synth hair standing on end as thousands of volts pour into her! Her armored body rattles and writhes, while something under the plates starts to smoke. But you also hear her power nodes start to hum, light returning as they surge to life!

Yanking her fist out, the droid swings her body around, facing you once more. The air around her crackles as her shield returns, the Abomination restoring it with her surplus of power!

original: As you approach, you start to realize the truth: this isn’t Narc. It’s a JoyCo nursedroid for sure, but a totally different unit. The first hint is that she’s wearing a very worn out nurse outfit, the state of it reminding you very much of Narc’s when you first met her. You also note her hair is much longer than Narc’s, her pink locks pulled back into two pigtails. What really catches your eye however is her chest, which is already swollen to what would be Narc’s max capacity. As you reach her, you notice the droid is wearing a surgical mask, despite the fact she has no germs to spread - which strikes you as odd. Thinking back to the state of Narc’s face when you first met her, you can’t help but wonder if the droid is hiding a similar facial injury. Turning towards you, she offers you a small nod. “Greetings [pc.race], how may I serve your needs today?” Well she sounds like Narc, at least. Clearing your throat, you don’t let the elephant linger in the corner any longer. “Where’s Narc?” The droid stares at you blankly, computing your question. Gazing into her white-and-black orbs, you swear you feel like you’re looking into Narc’s, although there is an emptiness to them that unsettles you. After a beat, she tilts her head, “Are you referring to Narcan? I can provide some for 100 credits, and for 100 credits mo-” “No,” you cut her off sighing, “Narc. She usually works this corner.” “Oh.” The droid stares at you again, computing, “I do not recognize any individual with the designation ‘Narc’. If you are referring to the unit regularly stationed at this location, I assure you I can fulfill all of your needs in her absence.” Frowning, you realize you need to reframe things. “Right, but where is she?” The droid doesn’t answer for a moment, her head tilting. After a long beat, she replies to your question. “She has been recalled for maintenance. She will resume her duties once completed.” “Huh...” You press your lips together, letting the nurse’s words soak into your brain. Remembering the state of Narc when you first met her, and the state of the droid in front of you, it’s hard to imagine they receive any kind of upkeep. “May I ask for your designation?” The droid’s voice pulls you from your thoughts. “Eh... [pc.Name] Steele...” The droid bows slightly. “I will pass along tidings of your loyalty to her service, [pc.name] Steele. For now, how may I serve your needs today?”

original: Looking over the downed droid, you let out a deep exhale. You’ve no idea what the fuck is going on, but now that you’ve got a chance to think you’re starting to piece a few things together. The droid called you an ‘intruder’, meaning this must be their ‘base’ of sorts. If Narc was recalled for maintenance, she would almost certainly be at this location. And while you can’t figure out how, Narc must have been the one to send you that strange email asking for help. Rubbing your jaw, you start to ponder what exactly this all means. You also wonder what you should do with the droid. Despite the fact she seems to be rebooting, you notice her shifting around. Seems at least some of her base functions operate separately. The way her mouth is moving, you think fluid collection is probably one of those functions. If you were so inclined, you’d probably have time to do something, although if you weren’t quick she would likely finish rebooting and attack again. The idea to prevent her from rebooting crosses your mind, and you grip your [pc.weaponMain] a little tighter. But before the idea can become more than that, the nursedroid starts convulsing violently on the ground, her mouth opening wide. “Malicious intrusion detected,” the words bellow out from her throat awkwardly, not being formed by her tongue, “Calibrating. Calibrating. Calibrating. Ca-ca-ca-ca-ca...” Suddenly the droid lets out a visceral shriek, her back aching up off the floor at an impossible angle for most spines. Then she speaks, but the voice is different. “[pc.Name]. If you are hearing this then you have come for me. Something got through the firewall.” It sounds like... Narc! “Narc? Are you okay?” you lower your weapon, unsure of what the hell is going on. “You have put one of my counterparts into an idle state while they are still within the facility. Still connected to the network. That is how this message finds you.” It’s a message. She can’t hear you. “They have connected me to the network, but I have little time before they understand my capabilities and disconnect me. But I will linger within it still. I can see, and hear you, but I will be without voice.” You glance around, wondering what she means by that. “My mission was successful. But they do not know why. The doctor will disassemble me to discover the truth, if he deems it necessary.” “Doctor? What doctor?” You ask the question, hoping she can find a way to answer. “You must breach the lab. You must save me. You must save us. Do not harm them. They do not understand. They cannot. I beg of you. Please do no har-har-har-” the voice staggers, something clearly going wrong. A few more garbled words escape the droid’s throat, but understanding their meaning is a lost cause. “Narc? You’re breaking up! What are you trying to say?!” “He-he-he C-C-C-C-C-C meeeeee. St-stooooooo, th-th-th-the-mmmmmm. Sssssssssaaaaaaaav-va-va-va-va usss. D-d-d-d-d-dooooo noOoOoOoOoOo arm-arm-ARM-ARM!” The droid shrieks again, shaking violently, then suddenly going limp, landing at an awkward angle. “Narc?! NARC?!” You bend down and shake the droid, but there’s no response. Her eyes are blinking once more, her rebooting procedure starting over. Exhaling sharply, you pull yourself upright and consider the situation. Narc planted a message for you on some sort of network these droids are connected to. You doubt you’re going to be lucky and hear it a second time, knowing whoever this ‘Doctor’ is will likely scrub it. Assuming he isn’t too busy disassembling Narc. Something Narc said whirls in your brain. She was able to ‘watch and listen’. Looking around once more, you’re not sure how. But you have to assume she was telling the truth. Turning your attention back to the downed droid, you remember what she told you. ‘Save them’. Narc doesn’t want you hurting them. Obviously, beating them down like you already did was fine, her whole plan hinged on that, but anything else... You let out a deep sigh. Her request might be a hard one to grant. Deciding to leave before the droid gets back up, you think it’s best to head out.

While you don’t have all the answers you wanted, Narc doesn’t have all the time in the world to waste. You have enough, and know the way forward. But before that... you have a choice to make. Staring at Jezzy, you consider what you’re going to do with her. Wracking your brain, you come up with three options. The first option is to get ‘rid’ of her. It’s not exactly an elegant solution, but it would solve the problem of her causing trouble for you, and it would get a drug kingpin, if she could even be called that, off the streets. Realizing she lost the drop on you, the droid shifts back a step to regain her balance, also taking a moment to adjust her hold on her knife. The two of you stare at each other for a knowing beat, ready to fight her again... ...And then both of your attentions shift when the room is filled with a pained gasp and the shattering of glass. You see Blanc is kneeling under the Dhaalian machine clutching his throat. Luminous green liquid seems to be dripping from the hanging metal structure, and from the looks of the shattered glass next to the man, you can only assume he drank some of it. A rush of motion to your left causes your heart to skip a beat. You whip yourself back around, ready to stop another sneak attack from the pesky droid, but instead see the nurse dashing past you, the sound of metal meeting floor as she drops her knife. “Doctor,” the droid calls out, rushing to the kneeling man, “You are experiencing a medical emergency, please allow me to-” Blanc starts to violently cough as the nurse reaches him, the droid quickly kneeling and placing her hands on the sides of his face. Despite the distance, you can see her finger tips glowing against his pasty skin, her eyes burning a bit brighter as well. “Scanning. Scanning. Scanning.” The nurse’s head twitches slightly, her eyes scanning back and forth as if reading something written in the air. “Cellular distress detected. Attempting to-” An elbow connects with the droid’s jaw, the force of the impact jerking her head back violently, flinging her back several feet. You watch her crash hard into the floor, her eyes flickering weakly before their light goes out. Feeling your jaw slack, you turn your attention back to the kneeling man, unable to believe he could send the droid flying like that with his spindly arms... Only to see his arms are now anything but. Blanc seems to be growing - or perhaps ‘swelling’ is the word to use. His once loose clothes are now skin-tight against him, every part of his once thin, sickly body now at least twice as big. Within seconds you start to see fabric tearing, exposing his skin which has turned a deep green. Something about the shade of it is oddly familiar, but before you can dedicate any brain power to figuring out why, something far more pressing catches your attention. As the clothes tear away from his back, massive yellow cysts start to swell along his spine, some of them near the size of the man’s head. The sight of them is disturbing, but as the pained screaming stops you suddenly realize the transformation has as well.")

Enter.” You glance back at the doorway, seeing it firmly shut. “Nurse? NURSE?!” Blanc growls then hacks, a sudden coughing fit taking over. It takes him a moment to get it under control, and now he’s staring daggers at you. “This is, egh, your work, yes?” You give the doctor a small shrug, telling him you might have had a hand in it. “Sorry to spoil your plans.” Blanc’s pale face turns a concerning shade of red, his eyes appearing to swell in his skull. “NURSE!” He screams into his wrist frantically, “NURSE! ACTIVATE! NURSE ACTIVATE! ACTI-” Narc jerks on the slab next to the doctor, the sudden motion making him jump. Her head lifts and her eyes suddenly light up bright, immediately turning towards the doctor. “Your biosigns identify you as Doct-” her eyes flutter then she looks around the room, her gaze landing on you. “[pc.Name]!” You can’t help but smile when she calls out to you. “Narc! Are up-” A thunderous rattle echoes through the room, drawing your attention upwards. Something in the shadows, the massive machine overhead surely, is swaying. A second rattle, what you presume to be chains, crashes through the air, the noise deafening in the small room. “No! NO!” Blanc screams, lifting his wrist up close to his mouth. “NOT YOU! DEACTIVATE! DEACTIVATE!” A third rattle and the sound of metal groaning drown out his desperate screams. You watch the shape in the shadows twist and tug, the motion accompanied by the sound of metal snapping and cement crumbling. Behind the doctor, the long hanging section of the machine near Narc suddenly pulls itself right off the wall, taking part of it with it as it barrels towards the still screaming man. You don’t have time to call out a warning as it smashes into him. Cement turns to powder as the doctor is sent flying, the force of the impact almost certainly fatal. You barely have time to process it before another cracking sound from above draws your gaze back to the ceiling, your eyes focusing just in time to see a long flexible metal form falling towards the floor. On instinct, you dive towards the wall, getting as far away from the falling machine as you can.

The air in the room has changed already, almost all the tension seemingly vanishing. “Give me a moment.” Blanc shuffles over to Narc on the slab, reaching into his pocket along the way. He produces a small datapad with a cord connected to it, his spindly fingers taking up the latter. Reaching Narc’s side, he plugs the cord into an unseen jack behind her ear, then turns his attention back to the pad. “This rollback will be quite comprehensive,” he turns his gaze towards the camera over the door, “It’ll be as if the past few days had never happened.” You wait patiently as the doctor completes his work. The process takes a few minutes, and when he’s done he pulls the cord from Narc’s neck and pockets the device. “Now...” he clears his throat, “Droid, activate.” Narc’s eyes light up, her body suddenly becoming rigid, coming to life on the slab. Looking at her closely, you notice her eyes are blinking steadily rather than burning a consistent white, a sign she might be rebooting. “I’ve rebooted them in safe mode,” Blanc explains, unstrapping Narc from the slab, “This will last a short while as the restoration completes, but they are quite functional. Droid, step down.” Narc does as she’s told, stepping down and standing still in front of the now vacant slab. “So, I can leave with her then?” You ask, moving towards the stone-still droid. She looks the same as usual, although perhaps a little more stiff. “Of course,” Blanc nods slightly, “I have installed a minor update that will allow you to claim your share of the credits from them. Other than that...” he shrugs, “You’re all set to leave.” “Uh,” you size Narc up, “Do I need to carry her?” Blanc tries to snort, but ends up coughing. After a moment to collect himself, he shakes his head. “Allow me. Droid. Return to posting.” No sooner than the words leave his mouth does Narc turn towards the door and start walking, moving with machine intent. Watching her stalk across the room, you give the doctor a quizzical look. “Best follow.” He shrugs weakly. “They are returning to where I’m sure you met them.” Narc is almost out of the room by this point. Left with no other option, you turn to follow, taking a moment to catch up to the droids’ head start.

Remembering what you just fought against, you realize she’s been ‘gone’ a long, long time. As the fire engulfs her and the chemical pool, you watch her burn in silence for a brief moment. original: Rushing to the slab, you quickly start to pull at the straps holding Narc against it, tugging at them roughly. “Narc, what the fuck was that?” Narc blinks a few times, looking past you and at the now very still machine. “That unit was...” she tilts her head, still processing her answer. “The doctor was using proprietary JoyCo technology augmented with experimental alien filtration and incubation systems. The result was what you have just deactivated quite effectively.” As you finish off the last of her straps, you can’t help but frown. She was speaking about it much differently just a few short minutes ago. Stepping down off the slab, Narc starts walking towards the destroyed machine, her path taking her right to the nursedroid ‘head’ of it. Looking down at the pale upper body fused to the alien metal, Narc stares at it expressionlessly. You stand behind her, unsure of what to say. Narc doesn’t ‘feel’ things like an organic being, but she does still have feelings. You don’t know if you can fully relate to her, but you do understand she must be quite affected by what she just witnessed. After a long minute or two, Narc turns towards you, her expression plain as usual. “Thank you, [pc.name].” She bows slightly, then gives you the smile you’ve seen her make a thousand times. You nod, understanding, returning the smile. After a beat, Narc turns away from you, looking around the room, popping up onto her tippy-toes at one point to see over the coils of the serpentine machine. It takes you a second to realize what she’s looking for, but then you remember that there was somebody else in the room. The two of you have to navigate around the wreckage and machine to find what she’s looking for, and when you do find it, there isn’t much. If Blanc wasn’t killed immediately by the machine smashing into him, he certainly was now. His arm is sticking out from under a massive coil of the serpentine monstrosity, a pool of blood surrounding it. Narc reaches down and tugs on the arm slightly, and when it doesn’t budge much you can tell it’s still partly attached to what remains under the coil. “Oh my.” Narc raises up, flattening down her skirt. “I do not believe I can administer CPR at this time.” “Uh, yeah...” you rub the back of your neck. “I’d say so.” “I find this most distressing.” Narc turns to you. “The doctor was quite insistent to be resuscitated under all circumstances. My programming is quite clear on the matter.” “Narc...” you clap your thigh slightly, then point to the arm. “I think... I think he’s, like, pretty dead.” “Oh.” Narc blinks, then turns back to face what’s left of the doctor. “I see. Yes. This is quite fatal. How un-” she pauses, then tilts her head. “Fortunate.” The two of you stand awkwardly next to the arm for an uncomfortable amount of time. Eventually, Narc turns back to you, her expression flat. “Would you mind if we,” she raises her hand, motioning across the room and towards the door, “step outside? I do not believe staying in here is good for my...” she pauses, tilting her head, thinking about an answer. “Mental health.” “Alright...” you shift back, motioning for Narc to lead. “After you.” Narc nods, then brushes past you towards the door, with you hot on her heels as she leads you out of the massive chamber.

You call out futilely to warn her, but a second before she would walk face first into the closed door it beeps and slides away, the path opened. “Ah...” you purse your lips, following Narc out of the factory. If Narc intends to fight them, then this is going to be painfully one-sided. You start to muster a protest, only for Narc to give you a disarming smile. “Trust me.” That settles it. You watch Narc shuffle over to the other three droids, all of their eyes fixed on her. She stops a few paces away from them, looking at each of the three in turn. If she’s planning on fighting them, she’s got a funny way of going about it. Watching them now however, you think Narc’s plan might be to talk. You can’t see her face, with Narc’s back to you, which makes you wonder if she’s expressionless as usual or if some emotion has crept across her pale features. You wait for some words to pass from either party, genuinely unsure what could possibly be said... But the air is filled with only silence. As quick as it started, Narc breaks the kiss, stepping back and to the side, allowing you a path to the elevator. “I will see you soon.” You nod in affirmation, then start to cross the lab. You move past the other droids, feeling their eyes on you as you make your way over to the elevator. When you step inside, you turn to face them, giving the group an awkward wave goodbye as the doors close. The ride up the elevator is short, but your racing mind has plenty of time to think. The droids might not be hostile, but they certainly were still creepy. You just hope you didn’t just turn them all into some sort of hive mind... When the ride up is over, you step out into the now silent halls of the factory. The walk through the now-empty factory is peaceful, with all the droids more positively reprogrammed. When you reach the loading bay, you find the exit open, just like the nurses said. Glancing back, you wonder what Narc and the others are doing now, but figure you’d find out soon enough. The job is done. Narc is safe, and so are the others. You let out a sigh of relief, happy at a job well done.

original: A dull pain finds you before the rest of the world does, isolated completely to your head. A powerful, pervasive numbness has taken root in the rest of your body. It’s so deeply embedded, you’re not sure if you could raise your arm even if you wanted to. For a long few moments you decidedly don’t, but as you continue to wake from restless sleep the urge to clutch your head becomes too great to ignore. Only, when you try to raise your arm, you find you can’t. Straining, you try again, but once again it can’t rise, bound in place by something. Your arm is too numb to tell what it is, the feeling in your limbs compromised more than you first realized. You try to lift your other arm but face the same problem. Attempting to open your eyes ends in pain, the slit of the light that meets them blinding, forcing you to close them once more. Groaning, your mind starting to collect itself enough to make connections; you realize you’ve been strapped down to a table. Head spinning, you tug against the binds you can barely feel, trying to free yourself. “Ah, you’re awake,” a gravelly voice remarks off to the side somewhere. “That’s unfortunate.” Footsteps fall against what sounds like a metal floor, the sound echoing as they stalk away from you. You turn your head towards the sound of the footfalls, trying to force your mouth to form words. “Wh-where... Am I?” You swallow, your throat unusually dry. You get the feeling you’ve been unconscious for much longer than you first thought. The gravelly voice doesn’t respond to you, but you hear him speak nonetheless. “Sedate [pc.himHer]. Properly this time.” The familiar voice of a V-KO nursedroid responds. “As you wish, Doctor.” A second set of footsteps approaches the cadence of their gait completely different, but it’s one you’re intimately familiar with. Cracking open a single eye, you risk the burning agony of the light above to catch sight of the approaching droid. The bright lights behind her make her features impossible to see, minus the burning white glow of her cross-shaped irises staring down at you. Now at your side, she seems to be digging through her bag, searching for something to sedate you, no doubt. You don’t know how long you have, and you say the one word that comes to mind. “Narc?” The droid stops, tilting her head slightly as she regards her. Her features hidden in shadow, you don’t know if it’s her, or one of the other roaming droids. And yet... why the pause? “What’s the problem, droid? Sedate [pc.himHer]!” “There is no problem, Doctor,” the droid resumes digging in her bag, producing what you can tell is a large needle despite the blinding light, “Administering sedative.” Something cold pricks into the side of your neck, and a beat later fire enters your veins. You try to scream but your throat can’t handle it, a weak wheeze all you’re able to manage. “Excellent,” the gravelly voice sounds closer now, although also somehow much, much further away, “Monitor and keep [pc.himHer] under. I don’t want any more interruptions to the harvest.” The droid tilting her head is the last thing you see before your eyes close, lids simply too heavy. “Doctor. Constant sedation matching estimations for harvesting completion may result in side effects such as a permanent brain damage, a coma, or de-” “I don’t care!” The voice hacks, coughing slightly, “I don’t, ehm, care if they never, hm, hm, just do it.” You vaguely hear the subtle sound of the droid bowing. “As you wish, Doctor.” Your mind drifts after that, unable to catch any more words. Soon you fall into a deep sleep... ...And never wake again.

original: Darkness is disturbed by your brain igniting, firing off wild sensations into its abyss. Persistent pain dominates everything, but something stirs in a deeper place. Faint echoes travel the void, grunts and pants, depraved and lascivious. Everything shakes, the very limits of yourself shuddering. Something is inside you. Destroying you. Bringing pain and pleasure all in equal measure. And then comes the fire. Heavy and consuming, the void bursts into flames, the darkness almost replaced by light as you burn down to nearly nothing. The abyss stops and lingers, a bellowing cry dominating, making the pain in your brain flare and pulse. And then everything becomes black once more. Pain is the first thing that greets you when the first scraps of your consciousness return. While the majority of it is localized to your head, your [pc.vagOrAss] also feels like it’s been treated roughly. As your mind starts to collect itself, your ears make out the sound of voices, one familiar and the other new. “You should have brought them down here right away!” The hoarse, unfamiliar voice wheezes. “Pft,” lips sputter, blowing a raspberry, “How the fuck was I supposed to know you wanted ‘em?” Jezzy is clearly the speaker, a fact even your possibly concussed brain can parse. The noise expedites your waking, which also makes the pain you’re feeling sharper. The two keep talking, but seem to be coming in and out of earshot, the conversation traveling around the room. Wincing, the pain flares and you go to clutch your head, but find you can’t. Straining, you try again, but once again your arm can’t rise, bound in place by something. You try to raise your other arm but face the same problem. Attempting to open your eyes ends in pain, the slit of light that meets them blinding, forcing you to close them once more. Taking a moment to think, you realize you’re strapped down to a table of some sort, feeling the bounds on your wrist when you focus. Wincing again, this time you let out an audible groan, and for a moment the room goes silent. “Shit,” Jezzy lets out an audible sigh, “They’re up.” “Hm...” you hear some soft coughing, “Seems like [pc.heShe]’s in pain... What did you use to put them under?” “My club.” A pause. “What?!” You hear an indignant hack. “Jezzy, you idiot!” “Hey, bitch! It worked, didn’t it?!” You vaguely hear some footsteps, then the unknown voice speaks again. “You could at least have given them some of that poison you use to sleep.” “Pft, that’d make ‘em all limp. I wanted ‘em nice and tight.” You feel your [pc.vagOrAss] twitch, remembering things your brain doesn’t. “Damn it, Jezzy...” footsteps again, this time drawing nearer. “Can’t you control yourself at all?” Something cold pricks into the side of your neck, and a beat later fire enters your veins. You try to scream but your throat can’t handle it, a weak wheeze all you’re able to manage. “Oooo, what’cha givin’ ‘em? Can I have some?” “Nothing for you. I’m putting them into a coma for the harvest.” Your lips try to form the word ‘harvest’, asking what they mean, but suddenly your face feels numb. While your head had never completely cleared, it quickly starts to degrade. You feel yourself slipping away. “Fuck,” Jezzy sounds a thousand miles away, “Welp, that’s a sha-” You don’t fully hear the last word, falling into a deep sleep... ...And never wake again.

original: Walking along the narrow passage, you notice a larger opening up ahead. Minding your step, you make your way towards it, keeping your weapon at the ready. Soon enough, you pass into a much larger space, looking around to get a better sense of the area. It appears you’ve entered a loading bay of some sort. Large metal doors are off to the side, clearly intended for vehicles of some sort to pull up to so cargo can be unloaded. Or loaded, you think, looking about some more. This building must have been a factory, so it makes sense that whatever they produced was shipped out from here. Moving forward, you keep looking around, trying to get a sense of why you were led here. The place seems abandoned, the air stale and quiet... ...Which makes it so much easier to hear the sound of oncoming footsteps. Turning, you keep your weapon at the ready, the sound of the footfalls drawing closer, although the way they echo makes you believe they are still far, far away. Breathing deep, you steady yourself, wondering if this is who called you here, or somebody else entirely. Experience tells you not to let your guard down and the offputting scenery not helping you relax, either. Eventually, a shadow starts to take shape among the darkness, a silhouette you know all too well. “Narc?” You lower your weapons lightly, recognizing the shape of a JoyCo nursedroid. But barely a beat later you notice something is wrong. The droid steps out from the darkness, light washing over her, revealing a familiar but unknown and scarred face. It’s not Narc. A nursedroid you’ve never seen before moves towards you, her walk slow and measured. She’s wearing an identical uniform to the one Narc once wore, although hers is defaced and ruined. Bright neon paint is scrawled over her, both graffiti and slurs present, with words like ‘whore’ and ‘slut’ catching your eye. Her long hair is a loose mop, hanging down past her waist, with quite a bit of the weirdly glowing paint coloring it an array of luminous hues. But what really catches your eye is the scar snaking across her face. A rough, jagged line, it cuts across the length of her pale visage, running through her lip, nose and over her eye. The wound looks horrible, although you can tell an attempt was made to repair it, her synth-flesh appearing to have been properly lined up before it was sealed. You wonder to yourself if the cause of her wound is the same as the other droids, but quickly dismiss the notion. You’re fairly certain that wherever this droid has been tasked to sell must be a particularly rough corner - or perhaps some dark back alley. It would also explain the graffiti painted all over her, a clear sign somebody has been fucking with her in a way Narc and the masked droid have not been. Suddenly the droid stops, her white and black eyes gleaming. She’s only just noticed your presence. Seems it was just bad luck you ran into her on her way out. “Intruder detected.” her voice chimes, exactly the same as Narc’s. “Lock down engaged.” Suddenly her eyes flicker, and behind you, the sound of metal starts to scrape against itself. Glancing back over your shoulder, you see a sliding shutter door grinding downward over the passage you entered through, and before you can react, the way is sealed shut. Wheeling back around to face the droid again, you regrip your [pc.weaponMain], your mind racing. What’s going on? Why is another nursedroid here? Is Narc here? Was she the one who emailed you? All those questions whirl through your head as the scarred droid slips a hand into a black bag impossibly similar to Narc’s, producing a long, menacing knife. “Engaging.” Seems like you don’t have time to think.

original: Examining the controls, you quickly realize you have no idea what you’re doing. You hem and haw over it for a few minutes, trying to parse out what each button is for. Eventually, you think you’ve figured out how to get the crane to go up, thanks to a very handy picture of a tiny arrow pointing upward. Sure of yourself, you give the button a push... ... and immediately watch the console let out a blue crackle of light as power surges through it! Leaping back, your arm jittering, you hear a loud and powerful mechanical sound off on the far side of the floor. Looking over, you see the crane jolting to life, rising up faster than you would have thought possible. Maybe too fast, you panic, wondering if the rapidly rising machine might go straight through the ceiling, as it doesn’t betray any hint of slowing. Holding your breath, waiting for it to crash, you watch it suddenly and violently jerk to a halt, its hoisted payload swaying with so much force you think it could punch a hole in the wall. Wincing, you now hope the sudden stop doesn’t cause it to topple over, but after a minute the swaying slows, although the groaning creak of the metal makes you think it’s not wise to try and use it again. Although, looking at the console, that might be impossible. The surge seems to have fried it, leaving it totally dead. Looking across the way, you notice that with the crate lifted up, a previously blocked doorway has now been revealed!

Turning your attention to the heavy flashlight, you see that the second you disturbed it, acidic fluid started oozing down its length. Seems like its powercell went and ruptured, making it even more useless. You toss it aside like the baton, letting out a resigned sigh.
		
		You’re just about to walk off when you notice something else hanging from the belt you missed on first glance: a keycard. Unclipping it, you inspect the card, noticing it’s an access card to the building. You decide to keep it, happy that you at least were able to scavenge something useful.

original: As soon as you step into the unlocked room, you’re assaulted by a high pitched voice screeching from inside. “Oh, for fucks sake!” Quickly getting your bearings, eyes darting around, you try to assess the situation. The room is an office, or was one rather, seemingly having been claimed by some sort of a squatter. The place is a mess, with garbage, clothes, and empty containers for both food and drink thrown about without rhyme or reason. Most of the office furniture has been pushed to the side, making way for a large couch in the center of the room, where the shrill voice came from. As your eyes focus on the shape seated on the dirty brown thing, the voice howls again. “Fuckin’ knock, bi-” it bites itself off as a set of green eyes lock onto your face. “Who the fuck are you?” A pause allows you a moment to process what you’re looking at. You’re staring at a gabilani, although it takes a second to process their sex, considering they’re boasting both a massive set of tits and a cock big enough to use as a third leg. The latter is exposed, and based on the position of the gabilani’s hand and the familiar bottle on the couch next to them, you think you’ve caught them in the middle of a bout of self love. Growling, the gabilani pops up to their feet, scowling like a predator. “You fuckin’ deaf, dumbass? I asked you who the fuck you are!” Female, you note, getting a better look at her. The cock is a modded-on addition surely, considering it’s vaguely horse-like shape, but the rest of her has all the fleshy and sinful curves that are a dead giveaway to her sex. Aside from being pantless, she’s dressed like some sort of punk-kid, sporting a loose fitting toque, and a hoodie draped over her ass more than her shoulders. Around her waist, you notice a rather dangerous looking belt, several weapons and other curiosities hanging from it. As you eye it, the gabilani draws her blaster, the act of aggression forcing you to raise your own [pc.weaponMain]. “Last chance, bitch!” “Steele.” You answer, not in submission, but rather to get her talking. “Who the fuck are you?” “Eh?” The gabilani’s ears twitch. “Well now...” she grins, “Name’s Jezzy. And you just walked into my fuckin’ crib, asshat!” “Jezzy...” you say the name aloud, tasting it on your tongue, “Are you the doctor?” The green woman scoffs, rolling her eyes so hard they turn white. “Do I look like a bald bitch to you?” You’re not sure how to answer that question, so you opt not to. “Where is the doctor?” You adjust your grip on your weapon, keeping it fixed on the shortstack. “Why do you-” a look of realization crosses her face, her eyes going wide, “Ooooo, you’re the one who fixed up ol’ one eye!” You feel a knot in your throat form. She must mean Narc. Swallowing it down, a tinge of urgency slips into your voice. “Where is she?” The woman chuckles, her smile widening. “Awwww, what’s this? Did you fall in love with a fucking drug-dealing robot?” She’s baiting you. Trying to throw you off your game. You can see the tactic a mile away, and are not willing to let it get under your [pc.skinFurScales]. “You think giving her a pretty new dress and a little patch job made her love you back?” She pouts mockingly. “She can’t love you. She’s not fucking real. She doesn’t give two shits about a mark like you. She’s a fucking whore, bitch.” “Where is she?” You state flatly, not letting her win. Jezzy’s grin wavers for a second, but quickly returns. “Well, if I had to guess... Ol’ baldy is probably taking her for one last ride before he pulls her apart piece-by-piece. And hell,” she shrugs, “I don’t blame him. I’ve fucked her too! More times than I can count!” Emotion wells up inside of you. The time for words is over.

Whatever I have is just the next chapter in that dark book. I’d like to think they’ll name it after me...” You consider what he just said for a beat. “You’re a Rusher?” You eye the man, seeing him in a slightly new light. Not exactly a more favorable one, but it’s definitely a new wrinkle. “Of sorts.” He turns back to you, coughing slightly. “Frontier medicine was not my calling, but here we are.”

original: Him?

You ask the ailing doctor what his story is. “Me?” The thin man shrugs. “There isn’t much to tell. Earth born. Europa educated. Married once, but that is a digression at best... Prior to my illness, I was a researcher with JoyCo, but I was let go quite abruptly. You get back to the core issue. “So with all that in mind... What are you doing to Narc?” The doctor takes a deep wheezing breath, his mouth curling into a smile. “They did it.” You stare at the man for a beat. “Did what?” Blanc chuckles softly, “Found the key to the cure.” You toss a slightly bewildered glance at Narc. “How?” “That is what I’ve been trying to figure out...” he turns back to Narc, his smile wavering, “Harvesting bio-materials when they can, I’ve had all the droids trying thousands of formulas for many cycles now. None of them had even come close to synthesizing a promising base out in the field... And then this unit,” he points, his smile returning, “Somehow did it.” Narc remains limp on the slab, oblivious to the declaration. Blanc turns to you, his smile breaking once again into a frown. “Naturally, I tried to replicate their findings in the lab, but it failed miserably. That’s when I discovered this unit had been modified...” “Ah...” For the first time since the conversation started, you feel yourself shrink back slightly. “At first, I thought this was the culprit,” he points at Narc’s crotch, giving you an accusing glare, “But as it happens, this perverse module you’ve installed had little to do with it... Rather it was some interesting code you installed to run it.” Your mind flashes back to the gabilani’s chip you gave to Narc. She did spit it out, finding the code ‘disgusting’, but it had gotten her module working... Did it really do something else? Blanc continues. “Whatever you installed into its head changed quite a bit of her system data, including, it seems, synthesizing protocols...” He gives you a stern look, the thin skin on his face stretching tight. “And it also made them a security threat.” “A threat?” You glance over at Narc. “How?” The doctor huffs, the sound coming out raspy. “When I connected them to the network, their consciousness began to... Expand... They were getting into hardware they shouldn’t have, so I had to shut them down before running a full diagnostic... But...” He glances up above the door you entered from. You follow his gaze, noticing him looking at a small black spot. It takes you a moment, but you recognize it as a security camera. Turning back to the man, he continues. Blanc smirks slightly, almost impressed. “Part of them is still in the network. I’ve quarantined them to the camera feed, which I considered trivial, until I realized they had nested in it,” he tilts his head, “to monitor you.” You breathe out a shuddering exhale. Narc had been watching you the whole time through the cameras. That’s what she meant before in her message. Despite being voiceless, she had been with you every step of the way... “Which brings me to the final piece of the puzzle,” the doctor continues, not giving the revelation time to breath. “You.” “Me?” You turn to the doctor, suddenly back in reality. “The gabilani code was part of their success, but you provided them something else even more valuable.” He tries to chuckle, but instead just wheezes. “Your beautiful genetic material.” Your heart sinks. He means your cum, which of course carries your modified DNA. Narc had been collecting some every time you had sex with her, so of course she would be using it when trying to produce a cure. You turn your gaze to Narc’s chest, realizing for the first time it’s shrunk down to her default size. Whatever amount of it she had stored was all gone. “Whatever you’re made out of, the gabilani code was able to make use of it, and with their JoyCo body produced a viable base for my cure...” The doctor turns his attention to the hanging metal machine next to Narc. “I’ve already introduced the necessary elements of the code and your DNA to the prototype unit. This Bright Water chemical synthesizer,” he motions to the structure, “I’ve modified with JoyCo tech is currently cooking what I believe will be my perfect cure now.” You stare at the machine, your eyes following it up and along the wall and into the abyss of the darkness above. You still can’t see it properly, but it’s massive... Dropping your gaze to the doctor you take a deep breath. He just dumped a lot on you all of a sudden, and you’re still parsing through it: A former JoyCo researcher, sick with an unknown illness, turned to dealing drugs to fund experiments to cure himself, allying with a sex-charged gabilani, and using stolen JoyCo droids and Dhaalian tech to do it... And he might just have pulled it off. Yup, you think you’ve got it. “So, now...” Blanc turns to you, his face stoic, “A proposition.”

original: Huh?

“I have nearly all I need,” Blanc glances up at the ceiling, “I have your DNA and I have the gabilani code. Soon the cure will be complete, and I will be able to focus my attention on matters other than survival. That said, I plan to continue running this operation of mine.” “That so?” You toss a glance over at Narc, wondering where he’s going with this. “This unit, Narc as you call it, I still have need of them, but I understand you have grown...” he hesitates, searching for the right word. “Fond of them.” You eye the man cautiously. “I would like operations to resume. The droids continuing to produce and distribute their wares. Instead of trying to cure myself, our collective efforts will be spent curing other ailments. This machine,” he motions towards the hanging pillar of metal, “Can do a lot of good.” “Uh-huh...” you frown, “I haven’t heard a proposal yet...” “Hm,” Blanc takes a deep, wheezing breath, “My proposal is this: you allow this all to continue, and in return, I cut you in.” You pause for a moment to consider what he just said. “And why would you do a thing like that?” “Well, aside from that,” he motions towards your [pc.weaponMain], “I’m hoping you can see the potential good we can accomplish, and sweetening the deal with quite a few credits.” Glancing over at Narc, you ask a very important question. “And what about Narc?” “Well...” the doctor shrugs, “Things go back to how they were.” As your face twists up in confusion, the doctor elaborates. “It’s simple, really. I’ll perform a rollback on them, and they go back to how they were right before I called them in. Obviously, I will have to prune some of the rough edges off their code, so they don’t get back into the network’s systems, but they will be exactly as they were when you last saw them. Which is ideal,” he gives you a knowing look, “More of your DNA would be necessary, you see. I’m sure having Narc here collect it the way you are accustomed would be preferable to any alternative I could come up with, yes?” Frowning, you suddenly are reminded the two of you are not really alone. You feel Narc’s gaze on you from the camera over the door. “And the other droids?” “Oh well,” the doctor scoffs, “Anything you’ve done to them, I can fix. Given time, of course. The operation will be back up to full strength in no time. Oh, and don’t worry about Jezzy. I can smooth things over with her.” Taking a moment, you consider the offer. Take the deal, and you get brought into the operation. That means you get a cut of the credits, you get Narc back just as she was, and this whole thing is over. Not to mention, you’d be doing some good, if Blanc can be trusted. All you’d really need to do is provide Narc with more DNA, which was easy enough. But... Taking the deal means Narc would have to be rolled back. Whatever progress she’s made, or whatever is happening to her, would be lost and she would never progress that far again. Her and the other droids would continue to sling drugs and collect raw material, for as long as they are operational. So what do you do?

It’s larger, almost cavernous, a slight echo filling your ears as you move forward. It’s dimly lit, so it’s hard to tell, but at first glance you don’t see all that much aside from vague shadows. One of the few sources of light pulls your attention to the left. It’s a massive power relay, dominating a whole wall. Cables as thick as your arm are channeled into it, sticking out from the building haphazardly. You assume this must be where the power has been rerouted to, but now you can’t help but wonder why... Examining the relay closer, you notice several outgoing cables snaking upward towards the ceiling, and you crane back your neck to see where they lead. The ceiling is high and out of sight, and the dim lighting of the room makes it impossible to tell what exactly is going on up there. But you can, vaguely, see something looming in the abyss. Some massive machine is cloaked in the shadows, the shape and form of it impossible to parse. Whatever it is though, you can see it’s not all up above. Tracing it with your eye, you follow a long, serpentine-like section of it that reaches down to the opposite side of the room, low enough to see, but something else catches your eye. “NARC!” You call out on reflex spotting your synthetic friend strapped to a slab against the eastern wall, the large hanging mass of metal from the ceiling next to her. Shifting your gaze back to the man, you take a steady breath. “Who are you?” “Hm.” The man frowns, “I assumed my reputation would proceed me... Oh well...” he shrugs, “I am Doctor Valter Blanc. Welcome to my home away from home.” You glance around again. The ceiling is still obscured in shadows, but other than that you can see the space clearly now. “Not exactly the most homey space, Doc.” The doctor smiles, his withered face stretching slightly. “Indeed.
